explanations.a: Glutamic acid is acidic but not the only acidic amino acid listed.
explanations.b: Aspartic acid is acidic but not the only acidic amino acid listed.
explanations.c: Correct. Both glutamic acid and aspartic acid are acidic amino acids.

questions.question: 2. Which of the following amino acids is NOT positively charged?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Histidine is classified as a basic amino acid.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Lysine carries a positive charge at physiological pH.
explanations.c: Correct. Aspartic acid is negatively charged, not positively charged.

answers.a: Must be provided in the diet
answers.d: Does not exist as a zwitterion

explanations.a: Correct. Essential amino acids cannot be synthesized in sufficient amounts and must be obtained from food.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Essential amino acids are not synthesized adequately by the body.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Amino acids generally exist as zwitterions.

questions.question: 4. Which is a basic amino acid with a positive charge?

explanations.b: Correct. Lysine is a basic amino acid carrying a positive charge.

questions.question: 5. When alanine and lysine form an amide bond, the product would be-

explanations.a: The product is a dipeptide, but a more specific name can be assigned.
explanations.b: Correct. When alanine is at the N-terminus and lysine at the C-terminus, the dipeptide is named alanyllysine.
explanations.c: Incorrect. This would represent the reverse sequence.
